title: Workout circuits (5-day push/pull/legs split, summer 2021)
html:
TL;DR

  • July 19, 2021 — Yahir lays down a 5-day push/pull/legs split sourced from a makeoverfitness.com PDF; pre-OSU summer between Brogden and freshman year.
  • Mon = push (chest/triceps/legs); Tue = pull (back/biceps); Wed = legs (general); Thu rest (implicit); Fri = push (shoulders/chest); Sat = pull (arms/light back/legs).
  • Standard hypertrophy ranges throughout: 3 sets of 8-12 on every lift, capped with a 1mi run/bike or 10-min stair walk for cardio.
  • Core circuit appended as standalone block: Russian twists, leg raises, heel touches, concentration crunches, windshield wipers, reverse crunches.
  • This is the earliest dated training-protocol in the vault — pre-dates the anabolic scoring frame Yahir later articulates by ~3 years; reads as the substrate that the later concept abstracts from.

What this surfaces

  • Yahir was already running structured templated routines at 17/18, copy-paste from a fitness site rather than improvising — pattern-match to the Orlando trip Google-Doc-as-template style and the Spanish lease template habit.
  • The hypertrophy-first program (no powerlifting compounds-only, no bodybuilding-style isolation-only) suggests aesthetic-recreational goals rather than performance — consistent with the post-COVID "regain" mode of summer 2021.
  • Pairs naturally with anabolic scoring as the what-it-evolved-into — and with the dad's house lockdown notes as the what-came-before.

Connections

anabolic scoring · burn bright die young · brogden · osu · the domestic yahir
